paper_threads.summary: RT sharing a comprehensive overview of 14 most important and influential types of JEPA (Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture) models including I-JEPA, V-JEPA, and Audio-JEPA variants.
RT discussing research showing that AI sycophancy is prevalent and consequential, particularly when discussing interpersonal conflicts with AI systems.
RT about a researcher's realization that LeCun was right and Elon was wrong regarding AI research approaches after transitioning from engineering to actual research.
RT sharing findings that AI sycophancy decreases prosocial intentions and promotes dependence in users.
RT about research showing how the Internet is being rewritten to serve AI agents and methods to measure this phenomenon, particularly post-ChatGPT.

announcements.summary: Teknium asks community if they'd be interested in a large data dump of agentic trajectories from Hermes Agent to train models for better performance in the system.
PlayStation announces the Vandal Runner Shell for Marathon, designed for hit-and-run tactics with fast movement abilities for repositioning.
Crunchyroll and Disney+ announce various anime releases and content updates including Re:Zero 4th season and World of Frozen at Disneyland Paris.
Weekly Shonen Jump releases new chapters including One Piece Ch. 1,178, Spy x Family Ch. 131, and multiple other manga series updates.

discussions.summary: jxmnop discusses the current state of AI automation, expressing skepticism about claims that we're months away from automating all white collar work.
fchollet explains AI capabilities using a chess analogy, discussing how humans would approach learning a completely new game like 'Glurg' from aliens.
teortaxesTex reports on apparent discrimination at Anthropic, sharing a translated Chinese post about someone being rejected for being insufficiently supportive of restrictions on open source AI.
mcmansionhell discusses the challenges writers face with plagiarism, noting that victims often face more consequences than plagiarizers and may even get blacklisted for speaking out.
tszzl discusses the realistic expectations for recursive self-improvement in AI, noting it will likely come in fits and starts with S-curves and waiting periods for new hardware.
